verses.1: First of all, then, I urge that requests, prayers, intercession, and thanksgiving be made in behalf of all people,
verses.2: for kings and all who are in authority, so that we may lead a tranquil and quiet life in all godliness and dignity.
verses.3: This is good and acceptable in the sight of God our Savior,
verses.4: who wants all people to be saved and to come to the knowledge of the truth.
verses.5: For there is one God, and one mediator also between God and mankind, the man Christ Jesus,
verses.6: who gave Himself as a ransom for all, the testimony given at the proper time.
verses.7: For this I was appointed as a preacher and an apostle (I am telling the truth, I am not lying), as a teacher of the Gentiles in faith and truth.
verses.8: Therefore I want the men in every place to pray, lifting up holy hands, without anger and dispute.
verses.9: Likewise, I want women to adorn themselves with proper clothing, modestly and discreetly, not with braided hair and gold or pearls or expensive apparel,
verses.10: but rather by means of good works, as is proper for women making a claim to godliness.
verses.11: A woman must quietly receive instruction with entire submissiveness.
verses.12: But I do not allow a woman to teach or to exercise authority over a man, but to remain quiet.
verses.13: For it was Adam who was first created, and then Eve.
verses.14: And it was not Adam who was deceived, but the woman was deceived and became a wrongdoer.
verses.15: But women will be preserved through childbirth—if they continue in faith, love, and sanctity, with moderation.
